question.text: A promoter holds 30% of the pre-issue capital of a company planning an IPO. Under Regulation 8A, what is the maximum number of shares this promoter can offer through OFS, expressed as a percentage of their pre-issue holding?

answer.text: 50% of their pre-issue holding (i.e., up to 15% of total pre-issue capital)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since the promoter holds 30%, which is more than 20% of pre-issue capital, they fall under the first category of Regulation 8A. They can sell up to 50% of their pre-issue holding through OFS. So 50% of 30% = up to 15% of total pre-issue capital can be offered for sale. **The Trap:** The 10% of pre-issue capital limit applies only to shareholders holding less than 20% of pre-issue capital. Applying this limit to a 30% holder is incorrect and demonstrates confusion between the two OFS limit categories. **Cross-Exam Context:** Promoter dilution calculations are tested in NISM Series IX and are also relevant in NISM Series VIII when understanding promoter pledge and shareholding disclosures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Map out all selling shareholders' holdings and calculate their individual OFS ceilings early. Remember that pre-IPO secondary transfers also count toward these limits.
